questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Consider a solenoid carrying current supplied by a DC source with a constant emf containing iron core inside it When the core is pulled out of the solenoid, the change in current will
questions.solution: When the core is pulled out the solenoid, the change in current will remain same.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A conducting rod of length $L$ lies in $X Y$-plane and makes an angle $30^{\circ}$ with $X$-axis. One end of the rod lies at origin initially. A magnetic field also exists in the region pointing along positive $Z$-direction. The magnitude of the magnetic field varies with $y$ as $B_0\left(\frac{y}{L}\right)^3$, where, $B_0$ is a constant. At some instant the rod starts moving with a velocity $v_0$ along $X$-axis. The emf induced in the rod is
questions.solution: Length along $Y$-axis would be $l \sin 30^{\circ}=\frac{l}{2}$ length $=L$ Velocity of rod $=v_0$, along $X$-axis variable magnet is field, $B=B_0\left(\frac{y}{L}\right)^3$, along $Z$-axis. Now, induced emf (formula) $$ E=B l v $$ where, $B, l_{\perp}$ and $v$ are perpendicular to each other, $l=L / 2$ Now, consider a small element of length $d y$ at a distance $y$ from origin along $Y$-axis, then emf of element $$ d E=B(d y) v_0=B_0 \frac{y^3}{L^3} v_0 \cdot d y $$ Emf of the rod, $$ \begin{aligned} & E=\frac{B_0 v_0}{L^3} \int_0^{L / 2} y^3 \cdot d y \\ & E=\frac{B_0 v_0}{L^3}\left\lceil\frac{y^4}{4}\right\rceil^{L / 2} \\ & E=\frac{B_0 v_0}{L^3}\left\lfloor\frac{(L / 2)^4}{4}\right\rfloor \Rightarrow E=\frac{B_0 v_0 L}{64} \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A rectangular loop of length $l$ and breadth $b$ is placed at distance of $x$ from infinitely long wire carrying current $i$ such that the direction of current is parallel to breadth. If the loop moves away from the current wire in a direction perpendicular to it with a velocity $v$, the magnitude of the emf in the loop is : ( $\mu_0=$ permeability of free space $)$
questions.solution:
We can show the situation as :
Since, loop is moving away from the wire, so the direction of current in the loop will be as shown in the figure. Net magnetic field on the loop due to wire $B=\frac{\mu_0 i}{2 \pi}\left(\frac{1}{x}-\frac{1}{l+x}\right)$ $=\frac{\mu_0 i l}{2 \pi x(l+x)}$ So, the magnitude of the emf in the loop $e=v B b=\frac{\mu_0 \text { ilvb }}{2 \pi x(l+x)}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The endpoints of a conducting string (shaped as a circular loop) of constant length are being pulled at a constant velocity v as shown in the figure. There exists a uniform magnetic field B in space which is perpendicular to the circular loop. If the loop always remains circular during the motion of its endpoints, then the emf induced in the loop at t=πR2v is
questions.solution:
At any instant, the radius of the loop r, will satisfy the following relation

2πr+2vt=2πR

⇒r=R-vtπ

The flux of the magnetic field at any instant, will be

ϕB=πr2B

So, the induced emf is

Ε=-dϕdt=2πBrdrdt

⇒Ε=2πBR-vtπv

⇒Ε=BRv
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The time constant of inductance coil is $3 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}$. When a $90 \Omega$ resistance is joined in series, then the time constant becomes $0.5 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}$. The inductance and the resistance of the coil are
questions.solution:
Time constant $t=\frac{L}{R}$
$3=\frac{L}{R}$ ...(i)
When a $90 \Omega$ resistance is joined in series the time constant becomes $0.5 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}$.
So, $0.5=\frac{L}{R+90}$ ...(ii)
From Eqs. (i) and (ii)
$\frac{3 \times 10^{-3}}{0.5 \times 10^{-3}}=\frac{R+90}{R}$
$\frac{30}{5}=\frac{R+90}{R}$
$6 R=R+90$
$R=18 \Omega$
This value put in Eq. (i)
$3 \times 10^{-3}=\frac{L}{R}$
$3 \times 10^{-3}=\frac{L}{18}$
or $\quad L=54 \mathrm{mH}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A coil having $n$ turns and resistance $R \Omega$ is connected with a galvanometer of resistance $4 R \Omega$. This combination is moved in time $t$ seconds from a magnetic flwx $\phi_1$ Weber to $\phi_2$ Weber. The induced current in the circuit is
questions.solution:
Given,
Number of turns in coil $=n$
We krsow that,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Current (i) }=\frac{e}{R^{\prime}}=\frac{-\Delta \phi}{R^{\prime} \Delta t} \\
& {\left[\because a=\frac{-\Delta \phi}{\Delta t}\right]} \\
& R=R+4 R \\
& i=-\frac{\left(\phi_2-\phi_1\right)}{(R+4 R) \Delta t} \\
& i=\frac{\left(\phi_2-\phi_1\right)}{5 R(\Delta t)} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Here, coil have n number of turns.
Hence, $\quad i=-\frac{n\left(\phi_2-\phi_1\right)}{5 R(\Delta t)}$
Induced current, $i=-\frac{n\left(\phi_2-\phi_1\right)}{5 R t}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A rod of length $80 \mathrm{~cm}$ rotates about its mid point with a frequency of $10 \mathrm{rev} / \mathrm{s}$. The potential difference (in volts) between two ends of the rod due to a magnetic field, $B=0.5 \mathrm{~T}$ directed perpendicular to the rod is
questions.solution:
Given, frequency of revolution of the rod, $f=10 \mathrm{rev} / \mathrm{s}$,
length of rod, $L=80 \mathrm{~cm}=0.8 \mathrm{~m}$ and magnetic field $B=0.5 \mathrm{~T}$
Since, rod is rotated about its mid point, hence radius of circular revolution,
$$
r=\frac{L}{2}=\frac{0.8}{2}=0.4 \mathrm{~m}
$$
$\therefore$ Potential difference between centre of rod and one end of rod due to revolution in magnetic is given by
$$
\begin{aligned}
V^{\prime} & =\text { induced emf }=\frac{\text { change in flux }}{\text { time taken }} \\
\frac{B . \pi r^2}{T} & =B . \pi r^2 . f \quad\left[\because f=\frac{1}{T}\right] \\
& =0.5 \times \pi \times(0.4)^2 \times 10 \\
& =0.8 \pi
\end{aligned}
$$

$\therefore$ Potential difference between two ends of rod due to revolution in the magnetic field, $V=2 V^{\prime}=2 \times 0.8 \pi=1.6 \pi$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The flux associated with a coil changes from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>35</mn><mo> </mo><mi>Wb</mi></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>79</mn><mo> </mo><mi>Wb</mi></math> within <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>10</mn></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>. Then the charge which flows in the coil, if resistance of coil is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
As, I=ER=dϕRdt
⇒Idt=dϕR
Integrating,
∫Idt=∫dϕR
⇒q=ϕR
If coil contains N turns, then q=NϕR.
If there is flux change Δϕ, then q=NΔϕR.
⇒q=17×1.35-0.79=0.08 C
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A rectangular loop of wire is placed in the $X Y$-plane with its side of length $3 \mathrm{~cm}$ parallel to the $X$-axis and the side of length $4 \mathrm{~cm}$ parallel to the $Y$-axis. It is moving in the positive $X$-direction with the speed $10 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}$. A magnetic field exists in the space with its direction parallel to the $Z$-axis. The field decreases by $2 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~T} / \mathrm{cm}$ along the positive $X$-axis and increases in time by $2 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~T} / \mathrm{s}$. The induced emf in the wire is
questions.solution: Induced emf in wire = Rate of change of flux $$ \begin{aligned} & =\frac{d}{d t}(B A)=A\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d t} B\right)+v \frac{d}{d x} B\right\} \\ & =12 \times 10^{-4}\left(2 \times 10^{-2}+10 \times 10^{-2} \times 2 \times 10^{-3}\right) \\ & =12 \times 10^{-4}\left(2 \times 10^{-2}+2 \times 10^{-4}\right) \\ & =24 \times 10^{-4}\left(10^{-2}+10^{-4}\right)=24 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~V} \\ & =0.000024 \mathrm{~V} \approx 0 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two parallel rails of a railway track insulated from each other and with the ground are connected to a millivoltmeter. The distance between the rails is one metre. A train is travelling with a velocity of $72 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$ along the track. The reading of the millivoltmeter (in $\mathrm{mV}$ ) is (vertical component of the earth's magnetic induction is $2 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~T}$ )
questions.solution:
Induced emf, $e=B v l$ where,
$\begin{aligned}
v & =\text { velocity of train } \\
& =72 \times \frac{5}{18} \\
& =20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& =2 \times 10^{-5} \times 20 \times 1 \\
& =2 \times 10^{-5} \times 20 \\
& =40 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{v} \\
& =40 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{mV} \\
& =0.4 \mathrm{mV}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In a uniform magnetic field of induction <math> <mrow> <mtext mathvariant="italic">B</mtext> </mrow> </math>, a wire, in the form of a semicircle of radius <math> <mrow> <mtext mathvariant="italic">r</mtext> </mrow> </math>, rotates about the diameter of the circle with angular frequency <math> <mrow> <mi>ω</mi> </mrow> </math>. The axis of rotation is perpendicular to the field. If the total resistance of the circuit is <math> <mrow> <mtext mathvariant="italic">R</mtext> </mrow> </math>, the mean power generated per period of rotation is
options: <math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext mathvariant="italic">B</mtext> <mi>π</mi> <msup> <mtext mathvariant="italic">r</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msup> <mi>ω</mi> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mn>2</mn> </msup> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>8</mn> <mtext mathvariant="italic">R</mtext> </mrow> </mfrac> </mrow> </math>

<math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext mathvariant="italic">B</mtext> <mi>π</mi> <mtext mathvariant="italic">r</mtext> <mi>ω</mi> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mn>2</mn> </msup> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>2</mn> <mtext mathvariant="italic">R</mtext> </mrow> </mfrac> </mrow> </math>

<math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext mathvariant="italic">B</mtext> <mi>π</mi> <mrow> <mtext mathvariant="italic">r</mtext> </mrow> <msup> <mi>ω</mi> <mn>2</mn> </msup> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mn>2</mn> </msup> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>8</mn> <mtext mathvariant="italic">R</mtext> </mrow> </mfrac> </mrow> </math>
questions.solution:
Magnetic flux linked


= BA cos ω t = B π r 2 cos ω t 2

∴   Induced emf e = - d ϕ dt = - 1 2 B π r 2 ω sin ω t

∴   Power = e 2 R = B 2 π 2 r 4 ω 2 sin 2 ω t 4 R

                            = B π r 2 ω 2 4 R sin 2 ω t

∵    < sin 2 ω t > = 1 / 2

∴    Mean power generated
                          = B π r 2 ω 2 4 R × 1 2 = B π r 2 ω 2 8 R .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A wire loop enclosing a semi-circle of radius R is located on the boundary of a uniform magnetic field of induction B→. At time t=0, the loop is set into rotation with velocity ω? about its axis 0, coinciding with a line of vector B→ on the boundary as shown in the figure. The emf induced in the loop is
questions.solution:
dϕ=B→·ds→=Bds

=B12ϕR2                (ϕ=Sector angle)

Magnitude of induced emf =-dϕdt

=B12R2dϕdt

=BR2ω2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A circular coil of radius $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ and resistance of $2 \Omega$ is placed with its plane perpendicular to the horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field. It is rotated about its vertical diameter through $180^{\circ}$ in $0.25 \mathrm{~s}$. If the magnitude of the induced emf is $3.8 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~V}$, then the number of turns of the coil is
(Horizontal component of earth's magnetic field at the place is $3 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~T}$ )
questions.solution:
Radius of coil, $\mathrm{r}=10 \mathrm{~cm}=0.1 \mathrm{~m}$
Resistance of coil, $R=2 \Omega$
Angle of rotation, $\theta=180^{\circ}$
Time of rotation, $\mathrm{T}=0.25 \mathrm{sec}$
Inducided emf, $\mathrm{E}=3.8 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~V}$
Horizontal Magnetic field, $\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{H}}=3 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~T}$ 
Induced emf is given as,
$\begin{aligned} & E=-\frac{N\left(\phi_f-\phi_i\right)}{t} \\ & =\frac{N\left(\phi_i-\phi_f\right)}{t} \\ & =\frac{N\left(B A \cos \theta^{\prime}-B A \cos \theta\right)}{t}\end{aligned}$
$\theta^{\prime}=$ Initial angle $=0^{\circ}$
Substitute necessary values,
$3.8 \times 10^{-3}=\frac{\mathrm{N}\left[3 \times 10^{-5} \times \pi \times(0.1)^2+3 \times 10^{-5} \times \pi(0.1)^2\right]}{0.25}$
on rearranging \& solving,
Number of turns $\mathrm{N}=504$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two long coaxial solenoids of radius R and 2R have equal number of turns per unit length. They carry time-varying currents i1=2kt and i2=kt respectively, in the same direction. A point charge released between the solenoids at a distance r, is seen to move along a circular path. Then the value of r is
options: It can be any value between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>R</mi></math>
questions.solution:
The magnetic field due to the inner solenoid (within its volume) is

B1=μ0n2kt

The magnetic field due to the outer solenoid (within its volume) is

B2=μ0nkt

The flux of magnetic field through the circular area of radius r is

ϕ=B1πR2+B2πr2

⇒ϕ=πμ0nkt2R2+r2

If E→ is the induced electric field at r, then

∮E→.dr→=-dϕdt

⇒E×2πr=πμ0nk2R2+r2

⇒E=μ0nkR2r+r2

The radius of the circle traversed by the charged particle is

r=mvqB2

v=Eqtm=μ0nkR2r+r2qtm

⇒μ0nkR2r+r2qtm=qrB2m

⇒μ0nktR2r+r2=rμ0nkt

⇒R2+r22=r2

⇒r=R2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A circular coil consists 70 closely wound turns and has a radius of $10 \mathrm{~cm}$. An externally produced magnetic field of magnitude $2 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~T}$ is applied perpendicular to the coil. The net flux through the coil is found to vanish when the current in the coil is $2.2 \mathrm{~A}$. The inductance of the coil is
questions.solution:
Given, number of turns in the circular coil,
$N=70, r=10 \mathrm{~cm}=10^{-1} \mathrm{~m}$
$B=2 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~T}$
$\theta=0^{\circ}$
Magnetic flux, $\phi=N B A \cos \theta$
$=70 \times 2 \times 10^{-3} \times \pi r^2 \times \cos 0^{\circ}$
$=140 \pi \times 10^{-3} \times\left(10^{-1}\right)^2$
$=140 \pi \times 10^{-5}$
$=140 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 10^{-5}=4.4 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~Wb}$
We know that,
$\phi=L I$
$L=\frac{\phi}{I}=\frac{4.4 \times 10^{-3}}{2.2}=2 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{H}=2 \mathrm{mH}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A conducting rod $P Q$ of length $1 \mathrm{~m}$ is moving with a uniform speed $2 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ in a uniform magnetic field of $4 \mathrm{~T}$ which is directed into the paper. A capacitor of capacity $10 \mu \mathrm{F}$ is connected as shown in the figure. Then, the charge on the plates of the capacitor are
questions.solution:
Charge on each plate should be
$$
\begin{aligned}
& q=C V \\
& V=v B l \\
& q=C(v B l) \\
& =10 \times 10^{-6} \times 2 \times 4 \times 1 \\
& q=80 \mu \mathrm{C} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
where,
So, charges on plates are $\pm 80 \mu \mathrm{C}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The energies required to set up in a cube of side $10 \mathrm{~cm}$
(i) a uniform electric field of $10^7 \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}$ and
(ii) a uniform magnetic field of $0.25 \mathrm{Wbm}^{-2}$ are respectively about
$$
\left(\mu_0=4 \pi \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{Hm}^{-1}, \varepsilon_0=8.9 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{Fm}^{-1}\right)
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A small square loop of wire of side $l$ is placed inside a large square loop of side $L(L>>l)$. If the loops are coplanar and their centres coincide, the mutual induction of the system is directly proportional to :
questions.solution:
Considering the larger loop to be made up of four rods each of length $L$, the field at the centre, i.e., at a distance $\left(\frac{L}{2}\right)$ from each rod, will be
$B=4 \times \frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{l}{d}[\sin \alpha+\sin \beta]$ i.e., $\quad B=4 \times \frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{I}{\left(\frac{L}{2}\right)} \times 2 \sin 45$ i.e., $\quad B_1=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{8 \sqrt{2}}{L} I$ So, the flux with smaller loop $\phi_2=B_1 S_2=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{8 \sqrt{2}}{L} l^2 I$ and hence, $M=\frac{\phi_2}{I}=2 \sqrt{2} \frac{\mu_0}{\pi} \frac{l^2}{L}$ or $\quad M \propto \frac{l^2}{L}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A current carrying circular loop is perpendicular to a magnetic field of induction $10^{-4} \mathrm{~T}$. If the radius of the loop starts shrinking at a uniform rate of $2 \mathrm{mms}^{-1}$, then the emf induced in the loop at the instant, when its radius is $20 \mathrm{~cm}$ will be
questions.solution:
Given, $B=10^{-4} \mathrm{~T}, r=20 \mathrm{~cm}=20 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}$
$$
\frac{d r}{d t}=-\frac{2 \mathrm{~mm}}{\mathrm{~s}}=-2 \times 10^{-3} \frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{s}}
$$

Induced emf, $\varepsilon=-\frac{d \phi}{d t} \Rightarrow \varepsilon=-\frac{d}{d t}\left(B \cdot \pi r^2\right)$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \varepsilon=-B \pi \cdot \frac{d r^2}{d t}=-B \pi\left(2 r \cdot \frac{d r}{d t}\right) \\
& \varepsilon=10^{-4} \times \pi \times 2 \times 20 \times 10^{-2} \times 2 \times 10^{-3} \\
& \varepsilon=8 \pi \times 10^{-8}=0.08 \pi(\mu \mathrm{V})
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A square coil ACDE with its plane vertical is released from rest in a horizontal uniform magnetic field B→ which extends over a length 2L as shown in the figure. The acceleration of the coil is
options: less than <math><mi>g</mi></math> for all the time till the loop crosses the magnetic field completely
less than <math><mi>g</mi></math> when it enters the field and greater than <math><mi>g</mi></math> when it comes out of the field
less than <math><mi>g</mi></math> when it enters and comes out of the field but equal to <math><mi>g</mi></math> when it is within the field
questions.solution:
There will be three situations,

(i) When the coil is entering into the field, flux increases and from Lenz's law the induced emf or current opposes the change. Therefore a<g .

(ii) When the coil is completely within the field, flux remains constant and hence no emf is induced. Therefore a=g .

(iii) When the coil is coming out from the field, flux decreases and from Lenz's law the induced emf or current opposes the change. Therefore a<g .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Find the potential difference between a and b, as shown in the below circuit.
questions.solution:
Equivalent for parallel combination of 5 Ω & 15 Ω is 

Req1=15×515+5=154 Ω

Similarly for three parallel resistor's equivalent 

1Req 2=124+112+18  ⇒  Req 2=4 Ω

Now current through 45 V branch,

I=45Req 1=4515/4=12 A

Voltage across a & b is

V=Req×I=4+10+154×12=213 V
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A long straight wire carrying current $I$ and a rectangular frame with side lengths $a$ and $b$ lie in the same plane as shown in the figure. The mutual inductance of the wire and frame is
questions.solution:
Magnetic field through the wire, $B=\frac{\mu_0 I b}{2 \pi a}$.
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore \text { Magnetic flux induced, } \phi & =\oint_s \mathbf{B} \cdot d \mathbf{a} \\ \Rightarrow & \phi=\frac{\mu_0 I b}{2 \pi} \int_a^{a+a} \frac{d a}{a} \\ \Rightarrow & \phi=\frac{\mu_0 I b}{2 \pi} \ln 2\end{array}$ $\therefore$ Mutual inductance of the wire and the frame is $$ M=\frac{\phi}{I}=\frac{\mu_0 b}{2 \pi} \ln 2 $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An artificial satellite with a metal surface is moving about the earth in a circular orbit. A current will be induced in the satellite, if
options: the plane of the orbit coincides with the plane of the equator.
the plane of the orbit is inclined to the plane of the equator.

the plane of the orbit coincides with the plane of the equator and the speed of the satellite is more than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo> </mo><mi>km</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>sec</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>.

the plane of the orbit coincides with the plane of the equator and the speed of the satellite is less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo> </mo><mi>km</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>sec</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>.
questions.solution:
According to Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction, EMF will be generated across the ends of the conductor when the magnetic flux through a loop of the conductor will change with respect to time.

When the orbit of the satellite is inclined to the equator of the earth, the distance from poles and hence magnitude of the magnetic field due to earth will change at different positions of orbit.

This causes a change in magnetic flux across the metal plate of the satellite and EMF will be generated.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The current I in an inductance coil varies with time according to the graph given in the figure.



Which one of the following graphs gives the variation of voltage with time across the inductor?
questions.solution:
The voltage across an inductor is

V=-Ldidt

So the voltage across the inductor will always be opposite to the rate of change of current.

When didt>0 then V<0 and vice versa

Also, the magnitude of V is proportional to the magnitude of didt.

When didtdecreases, V also decreases
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: A small square loop of wire of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>l</mi></math> is placed inside a large square loop of wire of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>L</mi><mo>></mo><mo>></mo><mi>I</mi><mo>)</mo></math>. The loops are coplanar and their centres coincide. The mutual inductance of the system is proportional to
options: lL
l2L
Ll
L2l
questions.solution:
Magnetic field produced by a current i in a large square loop at its centre,

B ∝ iL

Say  B = K iL
 
 Therefore, Magnetic flux linked with smaller loop,
     ϕ=B. S                                               '
     ϕ = k iL(/2)
Therefore, the mutual inductance

M =ϕi= Kl2L  
 
or         M ∝l2L

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: To measured a magnetic field between the magnetic poles of a loud speaker, a small coil having 30 turns and \(2.5 \mathrm{~cm}^2\) area is placed perpendicular to the field and removed immediately. If the total charge flown through the coil is \(75 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{C}\) and the total resistance of wire and galvanometer is \(0.3 \Omega\), then the magnitude of the magnetic field is
questions.solution:
Given,
number of turns in the coil, \(N=30\) turns
area of the coil, \(A=2.5 \mathrm{~cm}^2=2.5 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m}^2\)
total charge flowing through the coil,
\(Q_{\text {Net }}=7.5 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{C}\)
and total resistance of wire and galvanometer,
\(R=0.3 \Omega\)
We know that,
\(\text { net charge, } Q_{n e t}=\frac{\text { flux in the coil }}{\text { net Resistance }}\)
\(\therefore \quad Q_{\text {net }}=\frac{\phi}{R}\) ...(i)
We know that, flux \(\phi=\) magnetic field \(\times\) area
\(\times\) number of turns in the coil
or \(\phi=B N A\) ...(ii)
Now, from Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
\(\therefore \quad Q_{\text {Net }}=\frac{B N A}{R}\)
Putting the given values, we get
\(\begin{aligned}
7.5 \times 10^{-3} & =\frac{B \times 30 \times\left(2.5 \times 10^{-4}\right)}{0.3} \\
7.5 \times 10^{-3} & =\frac{B \times 7.5 \times 10^{-3}}{0.3} \\
B & =0.3 \mathrm{~T}
\end{aligned}\)
Hence, the magnitude of the magnetic field, \(B=0.3 \mathrm{~T}\).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: There is a pair of fixed, parallel rails of negligible resistance in a horizontal plane, at a distance of L=10 cm. The rails are connected, at one of their ends to an ideal battery of EMF 0.5 V, as shown in the figure. The system is placed in a vertically downward and perpendicular magnetic field of magnetic induction B=1 T. A metal rod of resistance R=10 Ω is placed perpendicularly on the rails. The rod and rails are frictionless. The magnitude of force, which is to be exerted on the rod in order to move it with a constant speed of 1 m s–1 in the direction shown, is
questions.solution:
εin=B⋅L⋅V=1×10×10–2×1

εin=0.1 V



I=0.5-0.110=0.04

F→=1×0.04×10–2×10

=0.4×10–2

|F→| =4×10–3 N

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The magnetic field of an electromagneticwave obeys the relation in a certain region is $B=10^{12} \sin \left(5 \times 10^6 t\right) \mathrm{T}$, where $t$ is the time.
Then, the induced emf, in a 300 tums in coil of area $20 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ oriented perpendicular to the field is
questions.solution:
Given, $B=10^{-12} \sin \left(5 \times 10^6 t\right) \mathrm{T}, N=300$ turns and area, $A=20 \mathrm{~cm}^2=20 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m}^2$
Hence, the emf induced
$$
\begin{aligned}
& e=-N \frac{d \phi}{d t}=-N \frac{d(B A)}{d t}=-A N \frac{d B}{d t} \\
& \Rightarrow e=-20 \times 10^{-4} \times 300 \times \frac{d}{d t}\left(10^{-12} \sin 5 \times 10^6 t\right) \\
& \Rightarrow e=6000 \times 10^{-4} \times 10^{-12} \times\left(\cos 5 \times 10^6 t\right) \times 5 \times 10^6 \\
& \Rightarrow e=-3 \times 10^{-6} \cos \left(5 \times 10^6 t\right) \mathrm{V}
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, the correct option is (2).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A metal $\operatorname{rod} A B$ of length $50 \mathrm{~cm}$ is moving at a velocity $8 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ in a magnetic field of $2 \mathrm{~T}$. If the field is at $60^{\circ}$ with the plane of motion as shown in the figure, then the potentials $V_A$ and $V_B$ are related by
questions.solution:
A metal rod $A B$ shown in the figure moves with speed $8 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$.
As the emf induced in the metal rod, $$ \begin{aligned} e_{A B} & =V_A-V_B=l \cdot(\mathbf{V} \times \mathbf{B}) \\ \text { But, } V_A-V_B & =l v B \sin \left(90^{\circ}-60^{\circ}\right) \\ V_A-V_B & =50 \times 10^{-2} \times 8 \times 2 \times \sin 30^{\circ} \\ & =8 \times \frac{1}{2}=4 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned} $$ Hence, the correct option is (b).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In the given electrical circuit, if the switch $S$ is closed then the maximum energy stored in the inductors is:
questions.solution:
In figure below, if switch $S$ is open then total energy stored in the capacitors, is suppose $E_0$.
Energy in IF capacitor, $$ E_1=\frac{1}{2} C V^2=\frac{1}{2} C\left(\frac{Q}{C}\right)^2=\frac{1}{2} \frac{Q^2}{C}=\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{4^2}{1}=8 \mathrm{~J} $$ Similarly, $E_2=\frac{1}{2} \frac{Q^2}{C}=\frac{2^2}{2 \times 2}=1 \mathrm{~J}$ So, the total energy, $E_0=E_1+E_2=8+1=9 \mathrm{~J}$ Now, switch's is closed then the common potential of Capacitors, $$ \begin{aligned} V_{\text {common }} & =\frac{C_1 V_1+C_2 V_2}{C_1+C_2} \\ & =\frac{1 \times 4+2 \times 1}{1+2}=\frac{6}{3}=2 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned} $$ Hence, now the new arrangement of energy, and $$ \begin{aligned} & E_1=\frac{1}{2} C_1 V_{\text {common }}^2=\frac{1}{2} \times 1 \times 4=2 \mathrm{~J} \\ & E_2=\frac{1}{2} C_2 V_{\text {common }}^2=\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times 4=4 \mathrm{~J} \end{aligned} $$ Now, from conservation of energy, Energy stored in the inductor, $E_L \simeq E_0-\left(E_1+E_2\right)$ $$ =9-(2+4)=3 \mathrm{~J} $$ Hence, the inductor has $3 \mathrm{~J}$ of energy.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Magnetic flux <math><mi mathvariant="normal">ϕ</mi></math> linked with a stationary loop of resistance <math><mi>R</mi></math> varies with time <math><mi>t</mi></math> as <math><mi mathvariant="normal">ϕ</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>T</mi><mo>–</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></math>. The amount of heat generated in the loop, during the time interval <math><mi>T</mi></math>, is
questions.solution:
|E|=dϕdt=(aT–2at)

H=∫E2Rdt=a2R∫0TT-2t2dt

=a2R∫0TT2+4t2-4Ttdt

=a2RT2t0T+43t30T-2Tt20T=a2T33R

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two concentric coplanar circular conducting loops have radii \(R\) and \(r(R \gg r)\). Their mutual inductance is proportional to
questions.solution:
Let us consider a coil having \(N_1\) turns and radius \(r\) is surrounded by another coil having \(N_2\) turns and of radius \(R\) as shown in the figure below,
Magnetic field due to coil 2 at the centre, \(B_2=\frac{\mu_0 N_2 I}{2 R}\) Magnetic flux through the coil, \(\phi_{B_1}=N_1 B_2 A=\frac{N_1 \mu_0 N_2 I \cdot \pi r^2}{2 R}\) But, \(\phi_{B_1}=M I\) From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get \(M I=\frac{N_1 \mu_0 N_2 I. \pi r^2}{2 R} \Rightarrow M=\frac{N_1 \mu_0 N_2 \cdot \pi r^2}{2 R} \Rightarrow M \propto \frac{r^2}{R}\)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Consider a toroid with rectangular cross section, of inner radius $a$, outer radius $b$ and height $h$, carrying $n$ number of turns. Then the self-inductance of the toroidal coil when current $I$ passing through the toroid is
questions.solution:
Given, a toroid with a rectangular cross-section of inner radius $a$ and outer radius $b$. Height of the solenoid $=h$ Magnetic field inside a rectangular toroid is given by $B=\frac{\mu_0 n I}{2 \pi r}$
using the infinitesimal cross-sectional area element, $d x=h d r$ $\therefore$ Flux passing through the cross-section of toroid. $\begin{aligned} \phi & =\int B \cdot d x=\int_a^b \frac{\mu_0 n I}{2 \pi r} \cdot(h d r) \\ & \phi=\frac{\mu_0 n I h}{2 \pi} \int_a^b \frac{1}{r} d r \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \phi=\frac{\mu_0 n h I}{2 \pi}\left[\log r l_a^b\right. \\ \phi & =\frac{\mu_0 n h I}{2 \pi}(\log b-\log a) \\ & \phi=\frac{\mu_0 n h I}{2 \pi} \ln \left(\frac{b}{a}\right)\end{aligned}$ Now, self inductance of rectangular toroid, $L=\frac{n \phi}{I}$ Putting the value of $\phi$, we get $L=\frac{\mu n^2 h}{2 \pi} \ln \left(\frac{b}{a}\right)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A conducting loop (as shown) has total resistance R. A uniform magnetic field B=γt is applied perpendicular to plane of the loop where γ is a constant and t is time. The induced current flowing through loop is
questions.solution:
Given,

Uniform magnetic field B=γt



Total flux, B1→. A1→+B2→.A2→

=Bb2cos⁡0o+Ba2cos⁡180o

=Bb2-Ba2

ϕ=B(b2-a2)

ϕ=γt(b2-a2)

We know that,

Induced current (i) =eR=dϕdtR

From Equation (i),

i=ddt[γtb2-a2]R

i=b2-a2γR

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A uniform magnetic field exists in a region given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mtext> T</mtext></math>. A rod of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> is placed along the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi></math>-axis and it is moved along the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis with constant speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mi> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. Then induced e.m.f. in the rod will be
questions.solution:
We know that

Magnetic field vector B→=3i^+4j^+5k^

length of rod L→=5j^

velocity vector v→=1i^

Induced emf is given as

E=L→.v→×B→

E=5j^⋅1i^×3i^+ 4j^+5k^

E=5j^.-5j^+4k^

E=-25 V
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A long solenoid of cross-sectional radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> has a thin insulated wire ring of uniform cross-section tightly put on its winding. The ring is made out of two different material such that one half of the ring has a resistance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> times that of the other half. The magnetic induction produced by the solenoid varies with time as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>t</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi></math> is a constant. The magnitude of the electric field strength in the ring (other than the induced electric field) is
questions.solution:
Let us assume that the current in the ring is i and the resistance of the two halves are r and 10r, then by faraday's law

dϕdt=πR2b=ir+i10r

⇒i=πR2b11r

The induced electric field in both the parts of the ring is

Eind=R2dBdt=Rb2



Let E be the field which is developed in each part due to charge accumulation at the junctions, then for the upper half

Eind+EπR=i10r

and for the lower half

Eind-EπR=ir

⇒2EπR-9ir=0

E=9ir2πR

⇒E=9r2πR×πR2b11r=922Rb
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A coil of area 10 m2 is placed in a uniform magnetic field of 0.3k lb. m-2, with its plane perpendicular to the field. The coil rotates at a uniform rate to complete the revolution in 8 s. find the average emf in mV. in the coil during intervals when the coil rotates from
i. 0° to 90° position
ii. 90° to 180° position
ii. 180° to 270° position
iv. 270° to 360° position
questions.solution:
Magnetic flux is given by ϕ=BAcosθ

e=-∆ϕ∆t=-ϕ2-ϕ1t2-t1

case i : for 0° to 90° position

e=-BAcos90-BAcos0t2-t1=0.3×102=32mV

case ii : for 90° to 180° position

e=-BAcos180-BAcos90t2-t1=0.3×102=32mV

case iii : for 180° to 270° position

e=-BAcos270-BAcos180t2-t1=-0.3×102=-32mV

case iv : for 270° to 360° position

e=-BAcos360-BAcos270t2-t1=-0.3×102=-32mV
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A magnetic field given by $B(t)=\left(0.2 t-0.05 t^2\right) T$ is directed perpendicular to the plane of a circular coil containing 25 turns of radius $1.8 \mathrm{~cm}$ and whose total resistance is $5 \Omega$. The power dissipation at $3 \mathrm{~s}$ is nearly
questions.solution:
Given that, number of turns, $N=25$
Radius of coil, $r=1.8 \mathrm{~cm}=1.8 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}$ 
Resistance of coil, $R=5 \Omega$
Magnetic field, $B=\left(0.2 t-0.05 t^2\right) \mathrm{T}$
Now, area of coil,
$\begin{aligned} A & =\pi r^2=3.14 \times\left(1.8 \times 10^{-2}\right)^2 \\ & =1.02 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^2\end{aligned}$
Induced emf, $\varepsilon=\frac{-N d}{d t}(B A)=-N A \frac{d B}{d t}$
$\begin{aligned} & =-N A \frac{d}{d t}\left(0.2 t-0.05 t^2\right) \\ \varepsilon & =-N A(0.2-0.1 t)\end{aligned}$
Substituting the values, we get
$\begin{aligned} \varepsilon & =-25 \times 1.02 \times 10^{-3}(0.2-0.1 t) \\ & =-2.55 \times 10^{-2}(0.2-0.1 t)\end{aligned}$
Now, instantaneous power,
$P=\frac{\varepsilon^2}{R}=\frac{\left[-2.55 \times 10^{-2}(0.2-0.1 t)\right]^2}{5}$
At, $t=3 \mathrm{~s}$, power dissipated is
$P=\frac{\left(2.55 \times 10^{-2}\right)^2(0.2-0.1 \times 3)^2}{5}$
$=1.30 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~W}=1.30 \mu \mathrm{W}$
It is close to option (d).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
